rulings.Case Title: SHARON WOODY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss, With Prejudice, Plaintiffs¿ Complaint Pursuant To Cal. Code Civ. Pro. § 583.410
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 15, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Westside Building Material Corp.¿s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410 and 583.420(a)(2)(B) is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless counsel for defendant notifies plaintiffs of this tentative ruling and they state that they do not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:30am in Department 602, Hon. Suzanne R. Bolanos presiding. Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/SRB)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Complaints In Intervention Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure §387
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 15, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Proposed Intervenors¿ motion for leave to file a complaint-in-intervention pursuant to C.C.P. 387 is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am in Department 602, Hon. Suzanne R. Bolanos presiding. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/SRB)
